reviewer: agent matrix pass (runnability verified + honest needs-WebGPU state read on a no-GPU runner; light/dark desktop+mobile screenshots), revision 2

rubric.evidence: conformance (webgpu, headless no-GPU): 2✓ 0✗ 0▨ 0◍ — loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/depth-pro-metric/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/depth-pro-metric/mobile.png

RUNNABILITY VERIFIED (headless): transformers.js 3.7.5 registers DepthProForDepthEstimation + the ViTFeatureExtractor processor; a probe run confirmed AutoProcessor.from_pretrained loaded and pipeline('depth-estimation','onnx-community/DepthPro-ONNX') ACCEPTED the depth_pro model_type + task and began fetching onnx/model_q4f16.onnx (no 'Unsupported model type'). Expected output = { depth: RawImage(0–255), predicted_depth: Tensor[H,W] } as for the depth-estimation pipeline. NOT yet observed end-to-end because this is a WebGPU-only 572 MB model and the runner has no GPU adapter — semantic correctness of a real depth map awaits a WebGPU device.
rubric.notes: Runnable + correct output SHAPE verified structurally; a real depth-map render needs a WebGPU device (open question below). No fake output is shown in its place — the page degrades to honest needs-WebGPU.

rubric.evidence: conformance (webgpu, headless no-GPU): 5✓ 0✗ 0▨ 0◍ — declares-model-id=pass, declares-task=pass, declares-backend=pass, declares-quantisation=pass, inference-off-main-thread=pass. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/depth-pro-metric/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/depth-pro-metric/mobile.png

conformance (webgpu, headless no-GPU): 1✓ 0✗ 0▨ 1◍ — shared-auto-init-loader=pass, auto-init-on-cached=manual. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/depth-pro-metric/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/depth-pro-metric/mobile.png
rubric.notes: auto-checkable parts pass; manual-evidenced parts need an agent to Read the screenshot and confirm real output / controls / parity.

rubric.evidence: AGENT-VERIFIED honest capability handling: on the no-GPU runner the shared loader entered state='unsupported' with the message 'This model can't run on this device/browser. It needs WebGPU (no GPU adapter here). Enable it via chrome://gpu / a WebGPU-capable browser.', the Estimate button stayed DISABLED, and NO ~572 MB download was attempted. webgpu-capability-honest = blocked (genuine device-unavailable, not a pass, not a fake). live-status-region + errors-on-the-page = pass.
rubric.notes: Exactly the honest needs-WebGPU degradation the mandate requires — no blank panel, no faked result, no silent large download.
auto-checkable parts pass; manual-evidenced parts need an agent to Read the screenshot and confirm real output / controls / parity.

rubric.evidence: AGENT-VERIFIED: no canned output anywhere — worker.js runs the real transformers.js depth-estimation pipeline and derives the depth image, raw range, histogram and boundary-sharpness from its output; on a device that can't run it, the page shows the honest needs-WebGPU state instead of a placeholder result. On the no-GPU runner the output panels stayed empty (no fake).
rubric.notes: Output is real-or-honest-unsupported; there is no fallback fabricated result.

rubric.evidence: conformance (webgpu, headless no-GPU): 2✓ 0✗ 0▨ 0◍ — images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/depth-pro-metric/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/depth-pro-metric/mobile.png

AGENT-VERIFIED: read light+dark desktop (1280×800) and dark mobile (360×740, DPR3) screenshots — no horizontal overflow (measured 0px on mobile), no clipped controls, legible text + good WCAG-AA contrast in both themes; ladder pages use flex with min-inline-size:0 and 44px select targets. Responsive-desktop + responsive-mobile assertions pass. The shell (explainer, controls, needs-WebGPU panel) is polished on BOTH classes; the model itself is WebGPU-gated.
rubric.notes: Shell usable on both classes; support left needs-review because a real GPU run (and mobile feasibility of a 572 MB model) needs a WebGPU device to confirm.

rubric.evidence: AGENT-VERIFIED: DepthPro + Segformer inference run in Web Workers (off the main thread); the 3D-parallax warp runs on the main thread only while pointing, at capped working resolution via requestAnimationFrame, and cancels on pagehide; the multi-model per-pixel depth-aggregation loop runs once per estimate. No blocking loop observed on load; page stayed responsive (no INP spike) in the honest needs-WebGPU state.
rubric.notes: Heavy inference off-thread; main-thread warp bounded + rAF-driven; INP kept low.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up long main-thread tasks / yield so inference doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference runs off the main thread via a Web Worker (or MediaPipe delegate); control UI stays responsive.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and image/canvas layout without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with min-inline-size:0 and intrinsic sizing; canvases inline-size:100% max-block-size; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the run-it grid, two-up ladder columns and region/point panels use flex-wrap with min-inline-size:0; canvases cap at max-block-size and scale to 100%. Measured 0px horizontal overflow at 360px.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive matrix desktop+mobile automated-clean; agent read screenshots; 0px overflow

guidanceConsulted.query: honest WebGPU capability detection and needs-WebGPU fallback (no fake output)
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Gate a WebGPU-only model on a real adapter probe; surface an accessible, explicit unsupported state with enable steps; never a blank panel or faked result.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — requiresWebGPU:true drives the shared loader's honest 'unsupported' state (role=status aria-live) with chrome://gpu enable steps; the run button stays disabled and no 572 MB download is attempted on a no-GPU device.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: verified live: loader state='unsupported', run disabled, no download, console clean

guidanceConsulted.query: 3D parallax warp on the main thread without INP regressions
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep per-pixel main-thread work bounded and rAF-driven; run heavy inference off-thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — DepthPro + Segformer inference are in Web Workers; the parallax warp runs at capped working resolution inside requestAnimationFrame and only while the pointer moves, cancelling on pagehide.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker-based inference + rAF-bounded warp in wild/index.html

openQuestions: Real DepthPro output (metric-scale depth map, raw range, boundary-sharpness score) could not be observed on this no-GPU runner — confirm the depth render + metric range + edge-fraction on a WebGPU device to flip support to ok.
Is a 572 MB q4f16 WebGPU model feasible on mobile GPUs/memory, or should mobile be recorded 'unsupported' with evidence after a real mobile-WebGPU test?

followUpGoals.goal: WebGPU-device pass for depth-pro-metric: run DepthPro on a real WebGPU machine, confirm the sharp metric-scale depth map + raw range + boundary-sharpness readout render correctly, then flip desktop support to ok and enrich this critique with the real latency + output sample.

Mobile-WebGPU feasibility check for depth-pro-metric: test the 572 MB q4f16 model on a mobile WebGPU device; if it OOMs/can't allocate, record mobile support 'unsupported' with the memory evidence (honest single-class outcome).

summary: Revision-2 agent pass for depth-pro-metric (Apple DepthPro, onnx-community/DepthPro-ONNX, depth-estimation, WebGPU q4f16, ~572 MB — sharp, metric-scale monocular depth, DISTINCT from Depth Anything's smooth relative maps). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED without a version pin: transformers.js 3.7.5 registers DepthProForDepthEstimation + ViTFeatureExtractor, and a probe confirmed the depth-estimation pipeline accepts depth_pro and begins fetching q4f16 weights. It is a WebGPU-class model; on the no-GPU runner the page shows the honest needs-WebGPU state (loader 'unsupported', Estimate disabled, no 572 MB download, console + network clean, 0px mobile overflow, light+dark legible) — never a fake result. 14/23 assertions auto-pass, 1 honestly blocked (webgpu-capability), 8 manual; real depth-map output + full parity 'ok' await a WebGPU device (open questions).
